'''Marits''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings brought by [[Human/Legends|Human]] colonists to the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Gaftikar]], a forested planet with vast grassy plains. They were [[Meter/Legends|meter]]-tall [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. The Marits had light beige scales that were slightly iridescent. They had sharp muzzles and small black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] with red slit-like pupils. The dominant members had very prominent red neck-frills. These dominant members were mostly [[Gender/Legends|female]], as Marit society was matriarchal. When Humans settled Gaftikar, the Marits were hired and brought to the planet to build the [[City/Legends|cities]] and their infrastructure. The Marits were selected because of their natural abilities that made them brilliant engineers: excellent visiospatial awareness, superb mathematical skills, and an aversion to wastefulness. When construction of the cities were completed, the Humans expelled the Marits from the new populated areas of the planet, effectively exiling the Marits in the wilderness.

The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] would eventually [[Battle of Gaftikar|intervene]], assisting with a coup to overthrow the Human government.

The Marits, natural [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]], impressed the commandos of Omega Squad when a group of them were able to rapidly assemble and transport the weapons given to them from the Republic, such as [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Web blasters]], without poring over a manual.

Marits were also able to disseminate information quickly throughout the populace. ARC N-11 discovered that the Marits were especially good at "cascading training." [[A'den Skirata]] would train ten Marits, they would train ten each, etc. until the information was shared with the entire force in a matter of hours.

It was custom for Marits to recycle the bodies of [[Death/Legends|dead]] family members by consuming them, usually in the form of cooked food, such as soup. This led to [[Clone commando/Legends|Clone commando]] [[Darman Skirata]] of [[Omega Squad]] advising his squadmates to remember to tell the Marits to not dig up and devour the bodies of two dead [[Covert Ops clone trooper]]s that Darman had killed in self-defense.
